title: Menu Loop — Persistent Interface
description: Wrap your menu in a do-while loop so the program keeps running until the user chooses Exit. Add file output using ofstream to save a product's data to 'inventory.txt'. The loop should repeat the menu after each action. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
test_code:
assert 'do' in cpp, 'Use a do-while loop to repeat the menu'
assert 'while' in cpp, 'The do-while loop needs a while condition'
cpp_no_space = cpp.replace(' ', '').replace('\n', '')
assert 'while(' in cpp_no_space or 'while (' in cpp, 'Include the while condition to check for exit'
assert 'ofstream' in cpp, 'Use ofstream to write product data to a file'
assert 'fstream' in cpp, 'Include <fstream> for file operations'
assert 'inventory' in cpp.lower() or '.txt' in cpp or '.dat' in cpp or '.csv' in cpp, 'Write to an inventory data file'
acceptance_criteria: Menu runs inside a do-while loop
Loop continues until user chooses Exit (option 6)
Uses ofstream to save product data to a file
Menu redisplays after each completed action
hints: do { ... } while (choice != 6); keeps the menu running until exit
A do-while loop always executes at least once, which is perfect for menus
Use ofstream outFile("inventory.txt"); to open a file for writing
Don't forget outFile.close(); or use it within a block scope
